instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `check-range` that evaluates value, low, and high each exactly once and signals an error unless low <= value <= high. Returns the value when valid.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro check-range (value low high)
  (let ((gv (gensym)) (gl (gensym)) (gh (gensym)))
    `(let ((,gv ,value) (,gl ,low) (,gh ,high))
       (unless (<= ,gl ,gv ,gh)
         (error "Value ~S out of range [~S, ~S]" ,gv ,gl ,gh))
       ,gv)))
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 X) (#:G2 0) (#:G3 100))
            (UNLESS (<= #:G2 #:G1 #:G3)
              (ERROR "Value ~S out of range [~S, ~S]" #:G1 #:G2 #:G3))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 X) (#:G113 0) (#:G114 100))
            (UNLESS (<= #:G113 #:G112 #:G114)
              (ERROR "Value ~S out of range [~S, ~S]" #:G112 #:G113 #:G114))
            #:G112)
